industries.name: Samarium-Cobalt Permanent Magnets
uses: Samarium-cobalt (SmCo) magnets in two compositions — SmCo₅ and Sm₂Co₁₇ — are the highest-performance permanent magnets for elevated temperature applications, retaining strong magnetic properties to 300–350°C versus ~150°C for NdFeB

SmCo magnets used in aerospace and defense: jet engine fuel flow meters, gyroscopes, inertial navigation systems, missile guidance actuators, and military communication equipment where NdFeB's temperature limitations are unacceptable
High-performance electric motors for industrial servo drives, robotics, and machine tool spindles where operating temperature and reliability requirements justify SmCo's 3–5× price premium over NdFeB
SmCo in medical devices: implantable drug infusion pumps, surgical tools, and MRI-compatible actuators — SmCo's corrosion resistance and temperature stability suit demanding medical environments

Traveling wave tubes (TWT) and magnetrons in radar and satellite communication transmitters use SmCo magnets for their focusing magnetic fields — military and satellite applications where high temperature and reliability are non-negotiable

companies.name: Arnold Magnetic Technologies
companies.use: Major SmCo magnet manufacturer supplying aerospace, defense, and industrial motor markets. Produces SmCo₅ and Sm₂Co₁₇ grade magnets for jet engine instruments, gyroscopes, and high-temperature servo motors.

companies.name: Electron Energy Corporation
companies.use: Specialty SmCo magnet producer with US defense certifications. Manufactures high-temperature SmCo magnets for military and aerospace applications including guidance systems, radar, and electronic warfare equipment.

industries.name: Nuclear Reactor Control & Research
uses: Samarium-149 (¹⁴⁹Sm) is a major fission product neutron poison in nuclear reactors — formed from ¹⁴⁹Pm decay, it has an enormous thermal neutron absorption cross-section (41,000 barns) and must be accounted for in reactor physics calculations

Samarium poisoning of nuclear reactors: ¹⁴⁹Sm builds up during low-power operation and can cause reactivity transients — nuclear engineers carefully manage Sm concentration during power changes and shutdowns

Samarium oxide (Sm₂O₃) as burnable absorber in nuclear fuel: incorporated into UO₂ fuel pellets to compensate for excess reactivity at beginning of fuel cycle — burns up (absorbs neutrons to form non-absorbing nuclides) as the reactor operates

Samarium-153 (¹⁵³Sm, t½ = 46.3 hours) radiopharmaceutical (Quadramet, Lexidronam) for palliative treatment of bone pain from metastatic cancer — targets hydroxyapatite in bone metastases, delivering beta radiation

companies.name: Lantheus Holdings
companies.use: Major radiopharmaceutical company. Produces ¹⁵³Sm-EDTMP (samarium lexidronam, Quadramet) for palliative treatment of bone pain in patients with osteoblastic metastatic bone lesions from prostate, breast, and lung cancer.

industries.name: Catalysts & Specialty Chemicals
uses: Samarium iodide (SmI₂, Kagan's reagent) as a single-electron reductant in organic synthesis — widely used in pharmaceutical synthesis for ketone and aldehyde reductions, radical cyclizations, and pinacol couplings that are difficult or impossible with conventional reducing agents

Samarium oxide as catalyst for dehydrogenation of ethylbenzene to styrene and for dehydration of ethanol to ethylene — samarium's redox chemistry makes it active for selective oxidation and reduction reactions

Samarium compounds in phosphor applications: Sm³⁺-activated phosphors emit in the orange-red region — used in specialty fluorescent lamps and phosphorescent materials for security printing and anti-counterfeiting applications
Samarium cobalt targets for physical vapor deposition (PVD) sputtering of SmCo thin films for magneto-optical recording and magnetic sensor applications

companies.name: Sigma-Aldrich (Merck KGaA)
companies.use: Major specialty chemistry supplier producing samarium iodide (SmI₂) and other samarium reagents for pharmaceutical and fine chemical synthesis. Kagan's reagent (SmI₂) is a standard tool in complex molecule synthesis.
